title: Fix handling of static keyword in methods
body:
Method declaration may have `static` keyword in two places. One is before `function`/`task` and it means that the method is static. `automatic` keyword can't be used in that place. The other place is after `function`/`task` and it means that the default lifetime of local function variables is static. There may be also `automatic` used.

Currently Verilator handles `static` keyword in both places as it was at the beginning of a declaration. This PR fixes it.

One thing is still wrong. It is the setting lifetime to ports. Currently a lifetime of a port is set to automatic if it is a port of a class method. Otherwise no lifetime is set:
https://github.com/verilator/verilator/blob/c241ec90ec9a3454678cec1b4e9cde08bed05158/src/V3LinkParse.cpp#L302-L308
It is incorrect. The section 13.3.2 of IEEE Std 1800-2017 says:
> Variables declared in static tasks, including input , output , and inout type arguments, shall retain their
values between invocations.

So we should assign a lifetime to all function arguments according to the default lifetime of `function`/`task`. I decided to leave it as it is for now, because it is a separate problem.
